statement.p: A vegetable garden will be surrounded by a frame of grass sod. The garden itself will be {19\ {\rm ft}} by {14\ {\rm ft}}\text{.} The widths of the four edges of the frame will be equal to each other. There is only {92\ {\rm ft^{2}}} of grass sod available, and it will all be used for this frame. How wide should the four edges be? (What is the value of x in the diagram?)

solution.p: We can find a formula for the surface area of the frame by taking the area of the outer rectangle and subtracting the area of the inner rectangle. The outer rectangles width is 19+2x feet. Its height is 14+2x feet. So its area is (19+2x)(14+2x) square feet. The inner rectangle area is (19)(14) square feet. So the surface area of the frame, in square feet, is (19+2x)(14+2x)-(19)(14)\text{,} which simplifies to {4x^{2}+66x}\text{.} So we must solve the equation {4x^{2}+66x}=92\text{.}
Writing the quadratic equation in standard form, {4x^{2}+66x-92}=0\text{.} Now we apply the quadratic formula.
The possible solutions are {-17.7927} and {1.29267}\text{,} but only {1.29267} makes sense in this context. Each edge of the frame will be about {1.29267\ {\rm ft}} wide.
